new-product.title: Define a new product
new-product.simple_explanation: You have an idea or opportunity, but the product still needs to be defined. The agent helps you turn evidence and conversation into product drafts.
new-product.when_to_use: Use this when there is no proven operating product and no single bounded feature is ready to start.
new-product.first_action: Open product/context.md and tell the agent the product name, owner, audience, and first business area.

steps.title: Set product identity
steps.goal: Replace placeholders with the product identity without inventing facts.
steps.prompt: Read product/context.md. Ask me for the product name, owner, initial audience, and first business area. Propose the smallest edits needed to replace TBD values. Do not invent facts.
steps.gate: The user confirms the identity draft.

steps.title: Define the problem
steps.goal: Describe an evidenced user pain or opportunity.
steps.prompt: Ask focused questions about audience, pain, frequency, urgency, alternatives, and evidence. Propose problem.md using the template, separate facts from assumptions, and list unanswered questions. Stop before approval.

steps.title: Define the vision
steps.goal: Turn the approved problem into a durable product direction.
steps.prompt: Read the problem and propose the product vision, intended outcome, audience, boundaries, and non-goals. Preserve traceability to the problem and call out contradictions. Stop before approval.

steps.title: Define decision rules and value signal
steps.goal: Make product trade-offs and user value explicit.
steps.prompt: Propose Product Principles, the North Star outcome, measurement notes, and guardrails. Keep principles separate from metrics. Identify decisions that require my input and stop before approval.
steps.gate: Approve Principles and North Star individually.

steps.title: Define the strategy
steps.goal: Choose the first scoped delivery bet.
steps.prompt: Propose strategy, target segments, trade-offs, metrics, roadmap, and delivery levels using the approved Foundation. Ask before making material scope decisions. Stop before approval.
steps.gate: Approve Strategy before modeling delivery domains.

rules: Read the nearest context.md before changing an artifact.
Write drafts only after showing the proposed change.
Never change status manually or create approval records.
Record assumptions, gaps, and decisions separately.
new-product.after: Read the pinned examples/events reference, then create one Domain -> User Goal -> Feature -> Use Case walking skeleton. Do not create a workspace until the feature is stable and registered.

existing-product.title: Document an operating product
existing-product.simple_explanation: A real product already exists. First describe what is observable today; only then decide where it should go next.
existing-product.when_to_use: Use this when users, releases, metrics, support, or operational evidence prove that a product is already running.
existing-product.first_action: Ask the agent to inspect repository and operating evidence before writing the Product Baseline.

steps.title: Describe the current product
steps.goal: Capture observed audience, value, capabilities, operation, signals, risks, and unknowns.
steps.prompt: Inspect the repository, runtime configuration, tests, user or support evidence, and operational signals. Propose Product Baseline with observed facts separated from inferred intent and unknowns. Cite evidence paths. Do not treat existing code as approved product scope.

steps.title: Define future direction
steps.goal: Turn the current baseline into explicit future bets.
steps.prompt: Read the approved Product Baseline and propose future bets, trade-offs, segments, metrics, and roadmap. Ask before inferring strategic intent from implementation evidence. Stop before approval.

rules: Separate observed current state from future intent.
Cite repository or operational evidence.
Do not rewrite product history or approval records.
Escalate to full Foundation when audience, value, or direction is uncertain.
existing-product.after: Read the pinned examples/events reference and model the first Domain -> User Goal -> Feature -> Use Case slice.

existing-documents.title: Bring existing documents into the framework
existing-documents.simple_explanation: Your main evidence is in documents. The agent reads them, records what each source says, proposes destinations, and keeps unmapped content visible.
existing-documents.when_to_use: Use this for PRDs, Jira exports, Confluence pages, wikis, roadmaps, ADRs, business rules, or architecture documents.
existing-documents.first_action: Read the import inventory and every source under product/knowledge/imports/sources/.

steps.title: Read the imported sources
steps.goal: Understand subjects, claims, decisions, rules, candidates, conflicts, and gaps before creating artifacts.
steps.prompt: Read every imported source and the inventory. Summarize subjects, problems, audiences, goals, features, rules, decisions, metrics, duplicates, conflicts, and missing information. Do not create artifacts yet. Ask questions about important ambiguities.
steps.gate: The user confirms that the source review is complete.

steps.title: Record source traceability
steps.goal: Create one explicit coverage record for every imported source.
steps.prompt: For every source, update traceability.json with review status, evidence locations, extracted claims, candidate ids, possible destinations, and gaps. Do not silently discard content. Every source must be reviewed, partially mapped, mapped, or marked not_applicable with a reason.
steps.gate: The user reviews coverage and unmapped content.

steps.title: Propose product drafts
steps.goal: Map useful claims to the right templates without assuming document semantics.
steps.prompt: Use the traceability ledger to propose draft mappings. Preserve source_documents in every mapping. Do not automatically convert Epic to Feature or Story to Use Case. Record conflicts, ambiguous ownership, and unmapped claims. Do not materialize or approve anything.
steps.gate: The user selects which mappings may be materialized as drafts.

steps.title: Materialize selected drafts
steps.goal: Create only the explicitly selected draft files.
steps.prompt: Summarize selected mappings, files to be created, source coverage, unresolved conflicts, and content that remains unmapped. Ask for explicit human authorization before running: spec-framework import materialize --product-root product --run <latest-run> --approved-by "Your Name" --yes. Never approve the resulting product artifacts.
steps.gate: Run import materialize only with explicit human identity and confirmation.

rules: Sources are evidence, not approved product truth.
Keep traceability separate from mapping proposals.
Never overwrite an existing product artifact.
Never create approval records during import.
existing-documents.after: Route each materialized draft to its normal owner, parent gate, template, and approval flow.

existing-feature.title: Start one bounded feature
existing-feature.simple_explanation: You already know the feature you want to deliver. The agent helps define its boundaries without forcing a full product-wide Foundation first.
existing-feature.when_to_use: Use this when one feature has a clear user, outcome, scope, non-goals, constraints, and target delivery.
existing-feature.first_action: Open foundation/feature-brief.md and describe the bounded feature to the agent.

steps.title: Define the Feature Brief
steps.goal: Make the user, outcome, scope, non-goals, constraints, signal, and delivery level explicit.
steps.prompt: Ask questions about the user, outcome, scope, non-goals, constraints, success signal, dependencies, and Delivery Level. Propose a bounded Feature Brief. Flag if the request is actually product-wide or too uncertain. Stop before approval.
steps.gate: Approve the Feature Brief before creating a workspace.

steps.title: Model the bounded delivery slice
steps.goal: Create one Domain -> User Goal -> Feature -> Use Case chain that fits the brief.
steps.prompt: Use the approved Feature Brief and the examples/events reference. Propose the smallest domain, goal, feature, and use case chain that delivers the brief. Preserve parent and non-ownership boundaries. Stop before approval.
steps.gate: Approve the relevant artifacts through their normal owners and parents.

rules: Keep the scope bounded to the Feature Brief.
Do not invent product-wide strategy.
Do not create a workspace before the Feature Brief gate.
Do not skip the walking skeleton.
existing-feature.after: Continue through Use Case, Specification, Design when applicable, planning, tasks, and delivery gates.

existing-implementation.title: Understand an existing codebase
existing-implementation.simple_explanation: Code exists, but it may be a prototype, legacy system, or unfinished implementation. The agent records what the code proves without treating it as product intent.
existing-implementation.when_to_use: Use this when code is the strongest evidence but there is no reliable proof of a live, well-defined product.
existing-implementation.first_action: Ask the agent to inspect code, configuration, data, integrations, tests, and operational evidence.

steps.title: Create the Implementation Assessment
steps.goal: Record observed behavior, architecture, data, integrations, tests, risks, and unknowns.
steps.prompt: Inspect code, configuration, database, integrations, tests, and operational evidence. Fill the Implementation Assessment with observed behavior, architecture, risks, and unknowns. Separate evidence from inferred product intent. Do not modify application code or create approvals.

steps.title: Recover product intent carefully
steps.goal: Use the assessment as evidence for the full Foundation sequence.
steps.prompt: Propose Problem, Vision, Principles, North Star, and Strategy one artifact at a time. Ask questions wherever implementation evidence does not prove user intent. Preserve the distinction between observed behavior and approved product direction.

rules: Code is evidence, not approved product scope.
Do not change application code during bootstrap.
Separate observed behavior from inferred intent.
Require the full Foundation before delivery work.
existing-implementation.after: Read the examples/events reference and continue through the first domain walking skeleton.

audit-only.title: Inspect without changing the product
audit-only.simple_explanation: This path is for diagnosis only. The agent reports gaps and risks but does not edit product files or advance gates.
audit-only.when_to_use: Use this when you want to understand the current state before authorizing any product mutation.
audit-only.first_action: Run validation and read the manifest, contexts, import runs, decisions, and existing reports.

steps.title: Inspect current state
steps.goal: Identify structural gaps, stale evidence, missing traceability, conflicts, and approval inconsistencies.
steps.prompt: Inspect the product state and report findings with paths and evidence. Identify the first blocker and its owning skill. Do not edit product files, materialize imports, approve artifacts, create workspaces, or write reports.
steps.gate: Audit findings are reviewed by a human.

steps.title: Prepare a safe handoff
steps.goal: Turn findings into an explicit remediation choice without executing it.
steps.prompt: Summarize the safest first remediation, required evidence, owning skill, exact command, and human decision needed. Keep the session read-only.
steps.gate: The human explicitly chooses whether to leave audit-only or start a supported mutation path.

rules: Keep the entire session read-only.
Do not create reports or approval records.
Do not infer approval from status fields.
Report evidence and uncertainty separately.
audit-only.after: Remain read-only until the human explicitly selects a supported starting point or remediation path.
